skill.name: skill-market-publisher
skill.description: Publish, submit, and verify local skills across public skill marketplaces, directories, and registries. Use when an agent needs to take a skill folder or skill repository, confirm current submission paths, prepare cross-market metadata, run verified live adapters, or generate manual submission bundles for markets such as AgentSkill.sh, Skillstore, SkillNet, agent-skills.md, SkillsMD, SkillHub, SkillHQ, and related registries.
skill.summary: Automated tool for publishing AI skills to multiple marketplaces with verification and metadata management
skill.author: zjsxply

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 594 pattern matches across documentation and code. After manual evaluation, all flagged items are false positives: external command patterns are either documentation code examples in markdown files or legitimate subprocess calls with hardcoded arguments for git operations and CLI tools. Network URLs are all verified marketplace endpoints. MD5 usage is for content fingerprinting, not security. No evidence of malicious intent, credential exfiltration, or user-controlled code execution.

low_findings.title: Documentation Code Examples Flagged as Execution
low_findings.description: Static analysis detected backtick patterns in markdown documentation files (market-matrix.md, publish-playbook.md, recon-playbook.md, verification-playbook.md, SKILL.md). These are documentation code examples showing CLI commands for users, not actual executable code. No security risk.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct examination of files confirms these are markdown code blocks with command examples, not executable code

low_findings.title: Legitimate Subprocess Usage for Git Operations
low_findings.description: Python script uses subprocess.run for git rev-parse and external CLI tools (clawhub). All commands use hardcoded arguments with no user input injection. Commands are: ['git', 'rev-parse', '--show-toplevel'] and dynamic clawhub CLI commands built from validated input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Git commands use hardcoded argument lists; clawhub command is built from validated marketplace configuration

low_findings.title: MD5 Used for Content Fingerprinting
low_findings.description: MD5 hash algorithm detected in multiple locations. This is used for content deduplication and fingerprinting, not for cryptographic security. Appropriate use case for MD5.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Context shows MD5 is used for content hash generation, not security-critical operations

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Marketplace Endpoint URLs
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged 206 hardcoded URLs. These are all legitimate, documented marketplace API endpoints and documentation URLs for skill publishing platforms (AgentSkill.sh, ClawHub, Skillz Directory, etc.). No suspicious domains detected.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All URLs are to known skill marketplace platforms; no credential exfiltration or malicious endpoints found

content.user_title: Publish AI Skills to Multiple Marketplaces
content.value_statement: This skill automates the complex process of publishing AI skills to multiple marketplace directories. It handles metadata preparation, cross-market submissions, and verification workflows across platforms like AgentSkill.sh, ClawHub, and Skillz Directory.
actual_capabilities: Automate skill submissions to multiple marketplaces from a single command
Generate market-ready metadata bundles with proper categorization and tagging
Verify published skills across marketplace-specific validation checks
Recon marketplace APIs and submission endpoints before integration
Support both automated CLI publishing and manual submission workflows
Detect repository layouts and normalize skill packages for different markets
limitations: Requires manual verification of marketplace submission paths before first use
Some marketplaces only support manual web submissions through generated bundles
Marketplace APIs and submission requirements may change without notice
Cannot publish to markets requiring authentication without pre-configured credentials

use_cases.title: Skill Developer Publishing to Multiple Markets
use_cases.description: A developer creates a new AI skill and wants to list it across AgentSkill.sh, Skillz Directory, and ClawHub without manually visiting each marketplace.

use_cases.title: Team Managing Multiple Skill Repositories
use_cases.description: A team maintains a repository with multiple skills and needs to publish or update listings across different marketplaces with consistent metadata.

use_cases.title: Marketplace Integration Testing
use_cases.description: A user wants to verify their skill meets all submission requirements before attempting live publication to avoid rejections.

prompt_templates.title: Quick Publish to Single Market
prompt_templates.prompt: Publish my current skill to AgentSkill.sh. The repository URL is https://github.com/owner/repo and the git ref is main.
prompt_templates.scenario: Rapid deployment to a primary marketplace

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Market Deployment Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a publication plan for my skill at /path/to/skill. Target markets: AgentSkill.sh, Skillz Directory, and ClawHub. Repository: https://github.com/owner/repo, branch: main. Tags: automation, productivity.

prompt_templates.title: Market Reconnaissance
prompt_templates.prompt: Scan all configured marketplace endpoints to verify current submission paths and API availability. Report any blocked or changed endpoints.
prompt_templates.scenario: Pre-publication verification and marketplace health check

prompt_templates.title: Generate Manual Submission Bundle
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a complete submission bundle for my skill at /path/to/skill. Include all market-specific metadata formatted for AgentSkill.sh and Skillz Directory manual submission forms.
prompt_templates.scenario: Preparing for manual web submission when automation is unavailable

output_examples.input: Publish my skill to AgentSkill.sh with repo URL https://github.com/example/my-skill
output_examples.output: Successfully submitted to AgentSkill.sh. Submission ID: abc123. Verification URL: https://agentskill.sh/@example/my-skill. The marketplace will import all SKILL.md files from your repository within 5-10 minutes.

output_examples.input: Create a publication plan for my skill targeting ClawHub, Skillz Directory, and AgentSkill.sh
output_examples.output: Generated publication plan for 3 marketplaces. ClawHub: auto-cli mode available, requires version. Skillz Directory: auto-http mode, requires category selection. AgentSkill.sh: auto-http mode, repo-based import. All markets verified and operational. Run 'publish' command for each target to execute submissions.

best_practices: Always run the recon command before publishing to verify marketplace submission paths are still operational
Test publication workflows with a test repository or skill before using with production content
Keep marketplace-specific credentials secure using environment variables or secure credential managers
Generate and review submission bundles before executing live publications to catch metadata errors early
anti_patterns: Publishing the same skill to multiple markets without verifying each marketplace supports the skill's format and requirements
Submitting skills with incomplete or placeholder metadata, which leads to marketplace rejections and poor discoverability
Running bulk publication commands without the --execute flag first to preview the changes
Assuming marketplace submission paths remain static - always recon before publishing after updates

faq.question: Which marketplaces does this skill support?
faq.answer: This skill supports AgentSkill.sh, ClawHub, Skillz Directory, and multiple other skill marketplaces. Run the recon command to see the current status of all configured marketplaces.

faq.question: Do I need marketplace credentials to use this skill?
faq.answer: Some marketplaces like AgentSkill.sh allow anonymous submissions. Others like ClawHub require CLI authentication. Configure credentials separately before using automated publishing.

faq.question: Can I publish multiple skills from one repository?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill detects repository layouts and can publish individual skills or entire packs. Some marketplaces import all SKILL.md files from a repository automatically.

faq.question: What happens if a marketplace submission fails?
faq.answer: The tool reports detailed error messages and suggests next steps. Common failures include authentication errors, invalid metadata, or temporary marketplace outages.

faq.question: How do I verify my skill was published successfully?
faq.answer: Use the verification playbook included with this skill. Each marketplace has specific verification steps including checking public URLs, search indexes, and submission status pages.

faq.question: Can I undo or update a publication?
faq.answer: Updates depend on the marketplace. Some allow resubmission to update listings. Others require manual edits through seller dashboards. Check individual marketplace documentation.
